blocks.content: The last two hours were different.

The road climbed. The trees changed — taller, darker, the kind that blocked out the sky along the edges and then released it, suddenly, into sweeping views that arrived without warning and departed the same way. Cassandra had her window down despite the cold, her elbow on the door, watching.
Ethan watched the road and the views in equal measure.
At some point she picked up her phone and held it up without warning.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: What are you doing.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Documenting the honeymoon.
She made a face. He glanced over, caught off guard, and she got him mid-expression — somewhere between confused and amused. She looked at the result and immediately started laughing.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Let me see that.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Absolutely not.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Cassandra.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: This is going in my camera roll forever. You look completely bewildered.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Because I was driving and you just —

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I'm keeping it.
She put the phone in her pocket, still smiling.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: If you could paint anything we've seen on this drive, what would it be?

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: The inn window last night. The mountain shape against the dark.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I'm going to choose to believe you were looking at the mountains.
He didn't say anything. She took that as her answer.
Another twenty minutes brought them to a roadside pullout with a hand-painted sign: OVERLOOK — BEST VIEW IN THE COUNTY. The county had strong opinions about this and was probably right.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Stop.
He stopped.

The view from the overlook was the mountains fully revealed — no suggestion, no dark outline, the whole range in the bright afternoon light: ridgelines layered behind each other into a distance that felt theoretical. Snow on the uppermost peaks. A river somewhere below, catching the sun. The sky an unreasonable blue.
They stood at the brick railing and looked at it for a while without talking. There was a particular quality to this kind of view — it made small talk feel wrong and real talk feel easy.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: An hour away.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Less than.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I keep thinking about what it's going to look like.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: The cabin?

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: We're going to walk in and I'm going to cry. I know I'm going to cry.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: That's okay.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You'll have to pretend you don't see it.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I will absolutely not be doing that.

She laughed. She was still looking out at the mountains when he leaned in and kissed her — no warning, no lead-up, just his hand coming up to her jaw and suddenly the view was gone and there was only him.
She went still for half a second. Then she leaned into it.
The wind moved through her hair. The mountains held still behind them.
When they came apart her eyes were still closed for a moment. She opened them slowly.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: What was that for?

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Nothing. Just wanted to.
She looked at him. Something warm and unhurried in her expression. Then she noticed his arm.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Did you have your phone out this whole time?

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: The light was perfect. The mountains were right there. I had about two seconds to make a call.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You planned that.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I absolutely did not plan that. I just didn't waste it.
She took the phone from him.
She looked at it. The brick railing, the mountains enormous in the background, the two of them caught mid-kiss in the afternoon light.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Sending this.
She sent it to the group chat without caption. The responses started almost immediately.
Now that's a picture. Save that one forever.
I'm framing this. Don't argue with me.
Okay I'm not crying, you're crying.
Also I'm SO jealous. Mountains, that view, that kiss?? Maybe somewhere like this is exactly what Nathan and I need 😉
MAYA.
What! I'm just saying the scenery is very romantic and I'm very observant.
I have no opinion on this conversation.
Maya I love you but we have a cabin to get to.
GO. Be happy. Details later.
Cassandra put her phone in her pocket and looked back at the mountains one last time.
Getting close.
The cabin will be ready. Drive safe.
She got back in the car. An hour away.
